title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the morning vs. afternoon pattern is a really helpful clue.

Is this delay affecting all users in your organization, or is it limited to specific people or teams?

- **All users** — everyone sees the delay
- **Specific teams or roles** — e.g., only admins, or only one department
- **Specific individuals** — just a few people
- **Not sure** — you've only confirmed it for yourself

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I've confirmed it with a few people on my team and they're seeing the same thing. I haven't checked with every single person in the org, but I'd guess it's everyone — at least the folks I've talked to all have the same complaint about morning emails being really late.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for checking with your team — that confirms it's a widespread issue, not account-specific.

The fact that this started about a week ago suggests something changed. Do you know if any of the following happened around that time?

- **A deployment or update** to TaskFlow or its infrastructure
- **A change in email provider or configuration** (e.g., SMTP settings, SendGrid/SES quotas)
- **A significant increase in usage** — new users onboarded, a big project kicked off, etc.
- **Not sure** — nothing obvious comes to mind

Even a rough sense of timing ("we onboarded 50 new users last Monday") would help narrow this down.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm not sure about any infrastructure changes — I don't have visibility into that stuff. But now that you mention it, I did start getting these new daily digest emails from TaskFlow about a week ago. Some kind of marketing or summary email? I think they started showing up right around the same time the delays began, but I'm not sure if that's related or just a coincidence. We didn't onboard a bunch of new users or anything like that.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That daily digest email is a really useful clue — the timing lines up well.

Here's my working theory: if TaskFlow started sending a new daily digest to every user, those emails likely go out in the morning (maybe around 8–9am). A large batch of digest emails hitting the mail queue at once could be crowding out the individual assignment notifications, which would explain why morning notifications are delayed the most and afternoon ones recover as the queue drains.

Does that match what you're seeing? Specifically:

- **What time do the digest emails typically land in your inbox?** (e.g., early morning before 9am, mid-morning, varies)
- **Roughly how many people are in your TaskFlow organization?** (This would tell us the size of the digest batch — 50 users vs. 5,000 makes a big difference.)

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: The digest emails usually show up around 9am, give or take. That lines up with when the delays are worst, so maybe you're onto something. We have about 200 people in the org. I'm not sure exactly how many get the digest but I'd assume it goes out to everyone.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours, likely caused by new daily digest batch saturating mail queue","problem":"Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed by 2-4 hours. The delays are worst for notifications sent around 9-10am and improve in the afternoon. The issue began approximately one week ago, coinciding with the launch of a new daily digest email feature. The problem is widespread, confirmed across multiple team members in a ~200-person organization.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The new daily digest email, sent to ~200 users around 9am, is saturating the mail queue or hitting rate limits on the email sending service. Transactional notifications (task assignments) are queued behind the bulk digest batch rather than being prioritized, causing a cascading delay that is worst in the morning and gradually clears through the afternoon as the queue drains.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a task to a user around 9:00-9:30am (when digest emails are being sent)","Observe that the assignment notification email arrives 2-4 hours late","Repeat the same assignment around 2-3pm and observe a shorter (but still present) delay","Compare against email sending logs to confirm the digest batch is queued immediately before the delayed notifications"],"environment":"TaskFlow instance with ~200 users. Daily digest email feature recently enabled (approximately 1 week ago). Email provider/SMTP configuration unknown to reporter — needs developer investigation.","severity":"high","impact":"All ~200 users in the organization are affected. Delayed task assignment notifications are causing people to miss deadlines because they don't learn about new assignments for hours. Morning assignments are most impacted.","recommended_fix":"1. Immediately investigate mail queue logs to confirm digest emails are contending with transactional notifications. 2. Separate transactional emails (task assignments) from bulk emails (digests) — either use separate queues, a priority system, or separate sending infrastructure. 3. If using a third-party email service, check if rate limits are being hit by the digest batch. 4. Consider sending digests during off-peak hours (e.g., 6am or overnight) or throttling digest sends to avoid queue saturation. 5. Long-term: ensure transactional emails always bypass or take priority over bulk sends.","proposed_test_case":"After implementing the fix, send a digest batch to all 200 users and simultaneously trigger a task assignment notification. Verify the assignment notification arrives within 2 minutes regardless of digest queue state. Repeat at 9am to confirm the morning delay pattern is resolved.","information_gaps":["Which email sending service or SMTP infrastructure TaskFlow uses (reporter lacks visibility)","Whether the digest feature was intentionally launched or is in beta/rollout","Exact mail queue architecture and whether it already has priority separation","Whether any email rate limits or throttling were triggered (visible in sending service logs)"]}
